text:
If you've been waiting for a stretch of low humidity and full sun, this is it, and it runs right through the weekend. Today tops out near 80 with a light north breeze, tonight settles into the low 60s, and Sunday climbs a touch further to the mid 80s with almost nothing to argue about between the models. The lake matches the mood, wind 5 to 15 knots today easing to light and variable by Sunday, waves a foot or less, water sitting comfortably at 74. Air's clean up here too, that moderate reading is a southern Vermont problem, not ours.

The catch is what's coming behind it. Heat and humidity build back in Monday, and by Tuesday the models start arguing hard, highs anywhere from the low 80s to near 90 ahead of a sharp cold front that could bring strong thunderstorms Tuesday night. Worth watching, not worrying about yet.

For now, get outside. Good swimming and paddling both days, light winds keep the lake calm through evening, and I wouldn't hesitate to plan a sunset sail Sunday.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-11T10:25:00+00:00): Mostly Clear, 61F feels 61F, humidity 88%, wind N 0 mph
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: Sunny, with a high near 80. North wind 3 to 12 mph.
  Tonight: Mostly clear, with a low around 61. Northeast wind 2 to 10 mph.
  Sunday: Sunny, with a high near 84. North wind 2 to 6 mph.
  Sunday Night: Mostly clear, with a low around 63. East wind 2 to 6 mph.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-11T06:19:00+00:00):
  1. Seasonable and dry conditions are expected today and tomorrow with less humidity.
  2. Heat and humidity will briefly build back in to start next week, peaking on Tuesday.
  3. A sharp cold front will pass south Tuesday night and Wednesday, and then bring more seasonable weather into the latter half of the week.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 217 AM EDT Saturday... No significant changes to the forecast.
LAKE (broad waters):
  Today: North winds 5 to 15 knots. Sunny. Waves around 1 foot.
  Tonight: North winds 5 to 10 knots, becoming light and variable. Clear. Waves 1 foot or less.
  Sunday: Light and variable winds. Sunny. Waves 1 foot or less.
  Sunday Night: Light and variable winds. Mostly clear. Waves 1 foot or less.
LAKE GAGE: water 74F, level 96.66 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-11: highs [GFS 79, Euro 77, ICON 79] spread 2F; precip chance [GFS 0%, Euro 0%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-12: highs [GFS 81, Euro 82, ICON 83] spread 2F; precip chance [GFS 0%, Euro 0%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-13: highs [GFS 83, Euro 89, ICON 88] spread 6F; precip chance [GFS 6%, Euro 10%, ICON 3%]
AIR: AQI 53 Moderate (PM2.5) — On Saturday, July 11th, expect moderate air quality in southern Vermont and generally good air quality in the north.
SUN: rise 2026-07-11T05:19-04:00, set 2026-07-11T20:37-04:00, UV max 7.2
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 82, low 58: Abundant sunshine. Highs in the low 80s and lows in the upper 50s.
  high 85, low 63: Mostly sunny. Highs in the mid 80s and lows in the low 60s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: Hotter and more humid weather will arrive to start the week. Monday will continue to be dry but more humid with highs in the mid to upper 80s. A First Alert Weather Day is in effect for Tuesday, at least for now, due to hot and humid conditions along with the chance for severe thunderstorms. High temperatures will reach the upper 80s to possibly the mid-90s. With dew points expected to be in the 60s and 70s, it’s possible for the heat index to be around 100 degrees. Also, a cold front is expected to bring afternoon or evening thunderstorms. These have the potential to be severe, though the timing is uncertain. We’ll continue to monitor the situation and alert you to any changes.
NBC5 FIRST WARNING (2026-07-10T22:28:00Z): Sunshine and low humidity expected all weekend — Turning hotter early next week Sunshine and low humidity expected all weekend Turning hotter early next week
